title: Write normalized records to local SQLite store
completion_summary: internal/store: SQLite-backed records table with schema version migrations. Columns: session_id, pattern_id (nullable, set later by detector), input_hash, input_text, output_text, system_prompt_hash, model_used, latency_ms, token_counts (JSON blob), created_at, user_message_id, assistant_message_id. UNIQUE(session_id, user_message_id, assistant_message_id) makes restart replays idempotent via ErrDuplicate. Indexes on pattern_id, session_id, created_at DESC. WAL mode + 2s busy_timeout for HTTP-reader vs pairer-writer contention. internal/pairing: per-session state machine — user starts a pending pair; new user overwrites; assistant with empty content (tool-call-only turn) is ignored; assistant with content closes the pair, looks up sessions.model + sessions.system_prompt for enrichment (cached per session), emits Record. Tool messages are ignored for v1. Live VM test against the 13 existing Hermes messages produced 3 paired records correctly: two 'say hi' single-turn pairs and the foundation-06 'Use the foundation-06-echo skill' → 'HERMES-APPRENTICE-FOUNDATION-06-MARKER' pair (the latter correctly skipping over 3 intermediate tool-call assistant turns and 3 tool result turns to find the final content). Restart idempotency: TestRecordsSurviveRestart unit + UNIQUE absorbs the cross-restart replay. Queryable by pattern_id: TestQueryFilterByPattern + store.QueryOpts.PatternID. 10 new unit tests pass. Deliverables: observer/internal/store/{store,migrations,store_test}.go + observer/internal/pairing/{pairer,pairer_test}.go.
acceptance_criteria: Schema includes: pattern_id, input_hash, input_text, output_text, system_prompt_hash, model_used, latency_ms, token_counts
